allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps BATNA compare alternatives in the full interacting system instead of a static snapshot of the negotiation.
allies.source_quote: This holistic view ensures the selected alternative accounts for interrelationships rather than just static snapshots.

allies.rationale_text: Scaffolding is an ally to BATNA because identifying a clear, manageable walk-away alternative creates an incremental framework for building negotiation competence — starting from the grounded fallback option and progressively constructing more sophisticated positioning.
allies.source_quote: Scaffolding | Enables Mastery: Choosing a manageable alternative or starting point allows for the gradual, incremental building of a complex structure, supporting the path from novice to expert.

antagonists.rationale_text: Curse-of-knowledge undermines BATNA by preventing the expert negotiator from accurately modeling what a walk-away alternative looks like from the other party's knowledge perspective — expert assumptions about shared context distort assessment of the fallback's attractiveness.
antagonists.source_quote: Inside view / motivated reasoning and curse-of-knowledge are explicit raw antagonists that remain deferred because the current target-id surface is still thinner than the prose.

antagonists.rationale_text: Anchoring undermines BATNA by making the first attractive option feel like the only serious alternative worth considering.
antagonists.source_quote: If the first available alternative is attractive, anchoring prevents exploring truly superior options.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Batna conflicts with optionality when maintaining fallback branches delays commitment to a bounded negotiated zone.
structured_tensions.source_quote: batna conflicts with optionality (optionality) when keeping fallback branches delays commitment to a negotiated zone with bounded upside.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Batna conflicts with commitment-bias when sunk negotiation posture blocks rational walk-away updates.
structured_tensions.source_quote: batna conflicts with commitment-bias (commitment bias) when sunk negotiation posture blocks rational walk-away updates.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Batna conflicts with confirmation-bias when negotiators assess the current deal and the walk-away alternative selectively rather than honestly.
structured_tensions.source_quote: batna conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when negotiators selectively evaluate options to confirm the attractiveness of the current deal rather than honestly assessing the walk-away alternative.

curation_notes.summary: BATNA remains somewhat proxy-derived on the ally/antagonist side, but its explicit structured tensions are strong enough to preserve without stretching the contract.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor status-quo or sunk-cost overlays beyond what the raw tension block already supports.
open_questions: Symphony, inside-view, motivated reasoning, and curse-of-knowledge remain source-real relation material but were left out rather than overfit to the current canonical id surface.

allies: scaffolding wired in this sweep. Symphony-style synthesis remains deferred — no clean canonical id for symphony.
antagonists: Curse-of-knowledge wired in Task 3.3 sweep via new canonical model. Inside-view remains deferred — no canonical model id.
